author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, Luke xviii. 1,’They ought always to pray, and not to faint,’ etc. And on the two who went up into the temple to pray: and of the little children who were presented unto Christ.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xviii. 1,’They ought always to pray, and not to faint,’ etc. And on the two who went up into the temple to pray: and of the little children who were presented unto Christ.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The lesson of the Holy Gospel builds us up unto the duty of praying and believing, and of not putting our trust in ourselves, but in the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What greater encouragement to prayer than the parable which is proposed to us of the unjust judge?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For an unjust judge, who feared not God, nor regarded man, yet gave ear to a widow who besought him, overcome by her importunity, not inclined thereto by kindness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he then heard her prayer, who hated to be asked, how must He hear who exhorts us to ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When therefore by this comparison from a contrary case the Lord had taught that “men ought always to pray and not to faint,” He added and said, “Nevertheless, when the Son of Man shall come, thinkest thou that He shall find faith on the earth?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If faith fail, prayer perishes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For who prays for that which he does not believe?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence also the blessed Apostle, when he exhorted to prayer, said, “Whosoever shall call upon the Name of the Lord, shall be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in order to show that faith is the fountain of prayer, he went on and said, “How then shall they call on Him in whom they have not believed?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then that we may pray, let us believe; and that this same faith whereby we pray fail not, let us pray.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Faith pours out prayer, and the pouring out of prayer obtains the strengthening of faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Faith, I say, pours out prayer, the pouring out of prayer obtains strengthening even for faith itself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that faith might not fail in temptations, therefore did the Lord say,” Watch and pray, lest ye enter into temptation.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Watch,” He saith, “and pray, lest ye enter into temptation.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is to “enter into temptation,” but to depart from faith?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so far temptation advances as faith gives way: and so far temptation gives way, as faith advances.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that you may know, Beloved, more plainly, that the Lord said, “Watch and pray, lest ye enter into temptation,” as touching faith lest it should fail and perish; He said in the same place of the Gospel, “This night hath Satan desired to sift you as wheat, and I have prayed for thee, Peter, that thy faith fail not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that defendeth prayeth, and shall not he pray who is in peril?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in the words of the Lord, “when the Son of Man shall come, thinkest thou that He shall find faith on the earth?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He spoke of that faith, which is perfect.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is scarce found on the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo! this Church of God is full: and who would come hither, if there were no faith?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But who would not remove mountains, if there were full faith?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look at the very Apostles: they would not have left all they had, have trodden under foot this world’s hope, and followed the Lord, if they had not had great faith; and yet if they had full faith, they would not have said to the Lord, “Increase our faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See again, that man confessing both of himself (behold faith, yet not full faith), who when he had presented to the Lord his son to be cured of an evil spirit, and was asked whether he believed, answered and said, “Lord, I believe, help Thou mine unbelief.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lord,” says he, “I believe,” I believe; therefore there was faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But “help Thou mine unbelief,” therefore there was not full faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But inasmuch as faith belongs not to the proud, but to the humble, “He spake this parable unto certain who seemed to themselves to be righteous, and despised others.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Two men went up into the temple to pray, the one a Pharisee, and the other a publican.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Pharisee said, God, I thank Thee that I am not as the rest of men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He might at least have said, “as many men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does, “as the rest of men,” mean, but all except himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I,” he says, “am just, the rest are sinners.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I am not as the rest of men, unjust, extortioners, adulterers.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, lo, from thy neighbour, the publican, thou takest occasion of greater pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “As,” he says, “this publican.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I,” he says, “am alone, he is of the rest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I am not,” says he, “such as he is, through my righteous deeds, whereby I have no unrighteousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all that I possess.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In all his words seek out for any one thing that he asked of God, and thou wilt find nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He went up to pray: he had no mind to pray to God, but to laud himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nay, it is but a small part of it, that he prayed not to God, but lauded himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: More than this he even mocked him that did pray.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But the Publican stood afar off;” and yet he was in deed near to God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The consciousness of his heart kept him off, piety brought him close.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But the Publican stood afar off:” yet the Lord regarded him near.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For the Lord is high, yet hath He respect unto the lowly.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But “those that are high” as was this Pharisee, “He knoweth afar off.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The high” indeed “God knoweth afar off,” but He doth not pardon them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear still more the humility of the Publican.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is but a small matter that he stood afar off; “he did not even lift up his eyes unto heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He looked not, that he might be looked upon.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not dare to look upwards, his conscience pressed him down: but hope lifted him up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear again, “he smote his breast.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He punished himself: wherefore the Lord spared him for his confession.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He smote his breast, saying, Lord, be merciful to me a sinner.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See who he is that prays.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou marvel that God should pardon, when he acknowledges his own sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus thou hast heard the case of the Pharisee and Publican; now hear the sentence; thou hast heard the proud accuser, thou hast heard the humble criminal; hear now the Judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Verily I say unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Truth saith, God saith, the Judge saith it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Verily I say unto you, That Publican went down from the temple justified rather than that Pharisee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell us, Lord, the cause.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I see that the publican goes down from the temple justified rather than the Pharisee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou ask why?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because every one that exalteth himself shall be abased, and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast heard the sentence, beware of its evil cause.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In other words, thou hast heard the sentence, beware of pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let now those ungodly babblers, whosoever they be, who presume on their own strength, let them hear and see these things: let them hear who say, God made me a man, I make myself just.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O thou who art worse and more detestable than the Pharisee!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Pharisee in the Gospel did indeed call himself just, but yet he gave thanks to God for it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He called himself just, but yet he gave God thanks.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I thank Thee, O God, that I am not as the rest of men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I thank Thee, O God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He gives God thanks, that he is not as the rest of men: and yet he is blamed as being proud and puffed up; not in that he gave God thanks, but in that he desired as it were no more to be added unto him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I thank thee that I am not as the rest of men, unjust.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then thou art just; so then thou askest for nothing; so then thou art full already; so then the life of man is not a trial upon earth; so then thou art full already; so then thou aboundest already, so then thou hast no ground for saying, “Forgive us our debts!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What must his case be then who impiously impugns grace, if he is blamed who give thanks proudly?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, lo, after the case had been stated, and the sentence pronounced, little children also came forth, yea, rather, are carried and presented to be touched.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To be touched by whom, but the Physician?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Surely, it will be said, they must be whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom are the infants presented to be touched?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To the Saviour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If to the Saviour, they are brought to be saved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom, but to Him “who came to seek and to save what was lost.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How were they lost?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As far as concerns them personally, I see that they are without fault, I am seeking for their guiltiness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence is it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I listen to the Apostle, “By one man sin entered into the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By one man,” he says, “sin entered into the world, and death by sin, and so death passed upon all men by him in whom all sinned.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let then the little children come, let them come: let the Lord be heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Suffer little children to come unto Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the little ones come, let the sick come to the Physician, the lost to their Redeemer: let them come, let no man hinder them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the branch they have not yet committed any evil, but they are ruined in their root.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let the Lord bless the small with the great.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the Physician touch both small and great.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The cause of the little ones we commend to their elders.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Speak ye for them who are mute, pray for them who weep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If ye are not their elders to no purpose, be ye their guardians: defend them who are not able yet to manage their own cause.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Common is the loss, let the finding be common: we were lost all together, together be we found in Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Uneven is the desert, but common is the grace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They have no evil but what they have drawn from the source: they have no evil but what they have derived from the first original.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not them keep them off from salvation, who to what they have so derived have added much more evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The elder in age is the elder in iniquity too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the grace of God effaces what thou hast derived, effaces too what thou hast added.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, “where sin abounded, grace hath superabounded.”
